for_c_level: Low risk posture with full pass on executed tests, confirming production readiness for TLS-off deployments; customer claims on federation reliability are viable. No regressions from prior runs, maintaining strong stability. Proceed with confidence to next release gating.
for_non_technical: Agents in this system can reliably share and recall memories across different computers without issues. Everything worked as expected in the tests, meaning the agents remember and access shared information correctly. One minor test report couldn't be read, but it didn't affect the overall success.
for_sme: All 34 executed scenarios passed without failures, impacting primitives like recall (S1,S1b,S4), linking (S11,S37), versioning (S9,S13), recovery (S14), hybrid search (S18), and namespaces (S35,S42). No failure modes observed; probable no root causes. Skipped S23 due to unparseable report.
headline: Hermes v0.6.2 fully passes federation tests in TLS-off mesh.
next_run_change: Fix scenario 23 report parsing before next campaign to achieve full coverage.
what_it_proved: Demonstrated reliable memory sharing, consistency, and feature stability in a multi-node setup without TLS, with all executed scenarios passing fully.
what_it_tested: Exercised 34 scenarios covering memory recall, deletion, linking, versioning, recovery, hybrid search, bulk ops, and namespaces across HTTP transport in a 4-node federation mesh with semantic primitives.
